items.title: USDA finalizes new SNAP retailer stocking standards (7 varieties per category)
items.summary: USDA’s final rule requires SNAP-authorized retailers to stock 7 distinct varieties in each of 4 staple food categories (up from 3), and to carry at least 1 perishable variety in 3 categories (up from 2). The rule takes effect July 7, 2026, and retailers must comply by Nov. 4, 2026.

items.title: USDA posts state-by-state timelines for SNAP food restriction waivers
items.summary: USDA’s waiver tracker lists multiple states restricting purchases like soda and candy with SNAP, with different start dates by state. Examples: Arkansas’ restrictions begin July 1, 2026; Colorado’s soft drink restriction is listed for Oct. 30, 2026; Hawaii’s soft drink restriction is listed for April 1, 2027.

items.title: KFF: CMS list names 8 states with 1115 waivers where some enrollees will be subject to Medicaid work requirements
items.summary: KFF reports that CMS released a list in June 2026 identifying 8 states with 1115 waiver programs that include individuals who will be subject to Medicaid work requirements starting Jan. 1, 2027. The states listed are Georgia, Tennessee, Wisconsin, Hawaii, Massachusetts, New York, Oregon, and Utah.

items.title: KFF: CMS Medicaid work requirements rule uses a more restrictive ‘medical frailty’ definition
items.summary: KFF says CMS issued an interim final rule on June 1 covering Medicaid work requirements, including the ‘medical frailty’ exemption. KFF’s analysis warns the rule’s more restrictive definition differs from states’ early expectations and may be hard to administer, increasing the risk of eligible people losing coverage.

items.title: IRS form update: maximum Additional Child Tax Credit (ACTC) is $1,700 per qualifying child for 2026
items.summary: IRS Form 1040-ES (NR) states that for 2026 the maximum Additional Child Tax Credit amount is $1,700 for each qualifying child. The same form also notes that to claim the CTC or ACTC you must have a valid SSN issued by the return due date (including extensions), with a special rule allowing only one spouse on a joint return to have a valid SSN.

items.title: IRS: 27 states opt into the Federal Scholarship Tax Credit program
items.summary: The IRS says 27 states elected to participate in the Federal Scholarship Tax Credit (FSTC) program. The program allows eligible taxpayers to claim a federal tax credit for qualified contributions to Scholarship Granting Organizations (SGOs) that provide scholarships for qualified K–12 education expenses.

items.title: CNBC: analysis says at least 3.5 million people lost SNAP access as 2025 law changes take effect
items.summary: CNBC reports an analysis finding at least 3.5 million people have lost SNAP access as changes in the 2025 reconciliation law took effect. The story cites a Congressional Budget Office estimate of $187 billion in SNAP funding reductions and says the law expanded work requirements to adults ages 55–64 and to some parents of minors ages 14 and older.

items.title: FAFSA (2026–27): students can file online now; use Federal Student Aid to track deadlines
items.summary: Federal Student Aid’s FAFSA page is the official starting point to submit the FAFSA online and confirms students should check federal, state, and school financial-aid deadlines (which can be earlier than the federal date). Filing early helps maximize eligibility for limited funds like some state grants and school aid.
items.source: Federal Student Aid (U.S. Department of Education)
